failure_summary: Gemma/Ollama failed to produce usable 12-candidate scoring output for Dr. Chemistry, then Kinetics entered the same retry path. Run was stopped after repeated zero-score failures.
protocol:
Batch Synthesis of 3,5-Disubstituted Isoxazole in TBAB/EG (1:5) DES
- Phenylacetylene (1a, 0.5 mmol, 1.0 equiv) and ethyl nitroacetate (2, 1.0 mmol, 2.0 equiv) were added directly to TBAB/EG (1:5) DES (1 mL) in an oven-dried 30 mL vial equipped with a magnetic stirring bar; no additional solvent, catalyst, base, or additive was used. The reaction mixture was stirred at 120 °C in an oil bath for 15 min, at which point full conversion (99%) to ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate (3a) was achieved via 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of the in-situ-generated nitrile oxide intermediate with phenylacetylene. After the reaction, the mixture was quenched with water, extracted with dichloromethane, dried over MgSO₄, and the solvent removed under reduced pressure; the isolated NMR yield was 83% (quantified against 1,3,5-trimethoxybenzene as internal standard).

batch_record.reaction_description: 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of in-situ-generated nitrile oxide with phenylacetylene to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
batch_record.raw_text:
Batch Synthesis of 3,5-Disubstituted Isoxazole in TBAB/EG (1:5) DES
- Phenylacetylene (1a, 0.5 mmol, 1.0 equiv) and ethyl nitroacetate (2, 1.0 mmol, 2.0 equiv) were added directly to TBAB/EG (1:5) DES (1 mL) in an oven-dried 30 mL vial equipped with a magnetic stirring bar; no additional solvent, catalyst, base, or additive was used. The reaction mixture was stirred at 120 °C in an oil bath for 15 min, at which point full conversion (99%) to ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate (3a) was achieved via 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of the in-situ-generated nitrile oxide intermediate with phenylacetylene. After the reaction, the mixture was quenched with water, extracted with dichloromethane, dried over MgSO₄, and the solvent removed under reduced pressure; the isolated NMR yield was 83% (quantified against 1,3,5-trimethoxybenzene as internal standard).

chemistry_plan.reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of in-situ-generated nitrile oxide with phenylacetylene to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
chemistry_plan.bond_formed: C-O and C-C bonds in isoxazole ring
chemistry_plan.bond_broken: N-O bond (partial, nitrile oxide generation from nitroacetate via dehydration)

feed_streams.reasoning: All reagents premixed in DES as a single feed. Phenylacetylene must be present when nitrile oxide forms in situ to trap it and prevent dimerization to furoxan. DES serves as both solvent and promoter. No incompatibility between phenylacetylene and ethyl nitroacetate at room temperature.

reagents.name: ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates ethoxycarbonyl nitrile oxide in situ at 120°C via thermal dehydration; 2 equiv used to ensure full conversion

reagents.name: tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.equiv_or_loading: 1:5 molar ratio with EG (1 mL DES total)
reagents.notes: Part of deep eutectic solvent; acts as reaction medium and may promote nitrile oxide formation

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal dehydration of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonyl nitrile oxide (EtO2C-CNO) in situ. The DES medium likely facilitates this through hydrogen-bond-assisted dehydration.

Concerted [3+2] cycloaddition of ethoxycarbonyl nitrile oxide (1,3-dipole) with phenylacetylene (dipolarophile) to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate regioselectively. The HOMO(dipole)-LUMO(dipolarophile) interaction favors the 3,5-disubstituted regioisomer.

stream_logic.reasoning: Single premixed feed. Both substrates are stable together at room temperature. Phenylacetylene must be present when nitrile oxide is generated (upon heating) to trap it immediately and suppress dimerization to furoxan. DES is viscous (~50-100 cP at RT) so preheating the feed line or using a slightly elevated feed temperature (40-50°C) may aid pumpability. Concentration: phenylacetylene at 0.5 M, ethyl nitroacetate at 1.0 M in DES.

chemistry_plan.mixing_order_reasoning: All reagents are premixed in the DES at room temperature before entering the heated coil reactor. This ensures phenylacetylene is present to trap the nitrile oxide as soon as it forms at 120°C, minimizing furoxan (nitrile oxide dimer) side product. No separate mixing stage is needed since the substrates are mutually compatible at ambient temperature.
chemistry_plan.deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species involved. The reaction proceeds thermally in air.
chemistry_plan.quench_reasoning: No inline quench needed. The batch protocol quenches with water post-reaction for workup purposes only. In flow, the product stream can be collected and worked up offline (water addition, DCM extraction, drying).
chemistry_plan.wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction; no photocatalyst or photochemical activation is involved.
chemistry_plan.confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism (well-established nitrile oxide cycloaddition). The DES viscosity (~50-100 cP at RT, lower at 120°C) is a practical concern for flow — may require wider-bore tubing (e.g., 1.0 mm ID) or preheating to reduce backpressure. The 2.0 equiv of ethyl nitroacetate is used because nitrile oxide generation is not quantitative (some decomposition/dimerization). Residence time of ~15 min at 120°C should translate well to a heated coil reactor. Pressure rating should accommodate operation above the boiling point of any volatile components (phenylacetylene bp 143°C, so 120°C is below bp — manageable). Uncertain whether DES viscosity will cause significant pressure drop issues in narrow-bore tubing; recommend 0.75-1.0 mm ID PFA coil with back-pressure regulator (5-10 bar).

steps.name: Kinetics & Residence Time
steps.summary: τ = 1.5 min, range [1.0–2.2] min, via class (IF = 10×)
assumptions: IF = 10× from default class median (no analogy data available)

steps.name: Reactor Sizing
steps.summary: V_R = 0.75 mL, d = 1.00 mm, L = 0.95 m, Pe = 17 (⚠ axial dispersion)
warnings: Pe = 17 < 100 — significant axial dispersion. Reactor behaves closer to CSTR than PFR. Reduce d (smaller d → higher Pe) or accept lower conversion efficiency.

steps.name: Mass Transfer
steps.summary: t_mix = 101.3 s, Da = 19.688 — Mass-transfer limited — mixing is the bottleneck

values.interpretation: Mass-transfer limited — mixing is the bottleneck

warnings: Da = 19.7 ≫ 1: severely mass-transfer limited. Need micromixer, smaller d, or pre-mixed streams.
assumptions: D = 1e-09 m²/s (typical for small organic molecules in liquid)

steps.name: Heat Transfer
steps.summary: Q_gen = 0.4430 W, Q_rem = 8.9997 W, Da_th = 0.0492 — Thermally safe

steps.name: Back-Pressure Regulator
steps.summary: BPR REQUIRED: 5.0 bar (Gas-liquid system detected — BPR mandatory to maintain gas solubility (Henry's law: C_gas = H·P) and prevent uncontrolled degassing.)
equations: C_{\mathrm{gas}} = H \cdot P_{\mathrm{partial}}\quad\text{(higher BPR} \Rightarrow \text{more dissolved gas)}

steps.name: Process Metrics
steps.summary: STY = 16.6000 mol/(L·h), P = 12.450 mmol/h, ṅ_lim = 0.0333 mmol/min, startup waste = 2.2 mL, IF = 10×

streams.pump_role: single feed containing all reagents premixed in DES
streams.reasoning: From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: All reagents premixed in DES as single feed. Phenylacetylene must be present when nitrile oxide forms in situ to trap it and prevent dimerization. Engineering calculations set reactor concentration at 0.0667 M and total flow rate at 0.500 mL/min.

pre_council_proposal.mixing_order_reasoning: From Chemistry Plan: All reagents are premixed in the DES at room temperature before entering the heated coil reactor. No separate mixing stage needed since substrates are mutually compatible at ambient temperature.
pre_reactor_steps: Preheat feed line to 40-50°C to reduce DES viscosity and aid pumpability
post_reactor_steps: Collect product stream for offline workup (water quench, DCM extraction, drying over MgSO4)
pre_council_proposal.chemistry_notes: Thermal 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition driven by in-situ nitrile oxide generation at 120°C. Engineering calculations show Pe = 17 (significant axial dispersion) and Da = 19.7 (mass-transfer limited), but single premixed stream eliminates mixing limitations. DES viscosity (~50-100 cP at RT) requires wider tubing (1.0 mm ID) and optional feed preheating. BPR required due to gas generation during nitrile oxide formation. No photochemistry involved - purely thermal process.

reasoning_per_field.residence_time_min: Engineering calculation estimated 1.5 min (range 1.0-2.2 min) based on 10× intensification factor from batch time of 15 min. No analogies available.
reasoning_per_field.flow_rate_mL_min: Engineering calculation: total flow rate 0.500 mL/min to achieve target residence time with reactor volume.
reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Same as batch (120°C) - required for thermal nitrile oxide generation from ethyl nitroacetate.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Engineering calculation: reactor concentration 0.0667 M based on limiting reagent flow rate and total flow.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Engineering calculation: BPR required at 5.0 bar due to gas generation during nitrile oxide formation (Henry's law considerations).
reasoning_per_field.reactor_type: Chemistry Plan specifies coil reactor for thermal reaction.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: PFA chosen for high temperature operation (120°C) and chemical compatibility with DES.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: Engineering calculation: 1.0 mm ID to handle DES viscosity while maintaining reasonable pressure drop (0.003 bar).
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: Engineering calculation: 0.75 mL reactor volume (d=1.0mm, L=0.95m) to achieve target residence time.
reasoning_per_field.wavelength_nm: Chemistry Plan confirms no photochemistry - purely thermal reaction.
reasoning_per_field.deoxygenation_method: Chemistry Plan confirms no oxygen sensitivity - reaction proceeds in air.
